notes: A single turn aborts MID-INFERENCE and settles back to ready with no normal turn-end signal, proving the session does NOT stick in `working` after the abort. Elicited deterministically by a CONTEXT-WINDOW OVERFLOW of the model OUTPUT: the local LM Studio model is loaded at n_ctx 22528, just above the ~21.4k prompt opencode builds in run-cell.sh's NON-git staging cwd (opencode enumerates the directory tree when there is no git repo, inflating the system prompt), leaving ~1.1k output headroom; a prompt demanding a 12000-word essay with no tools overflows the window mid-generation and opencode persists a terminal `step-finish` part with reason="length". parser.go maps reason=length → turn_done (parser.go:179) and the watcher's isTerminalPart accepts reason=length (watcher.go:435), so BOTH the live daemon and the replay fixture observe the working→ready settle — and the driver's unified oc_turn_total counter (which counts reason IN stop/interrupted/length/error/content-filter) lets wait_turn detect the boundary. This is a ≥1-part terminal abort that needs no remote provider, no error-injection, and no message.data.error carrier — the parts-only transcript export already carries the step-finish part. The recipe drives the LONG-LIVED opencode TUI (a `{type:live}` marker) rather than a headless `opencode run`: a headless run self-exits the instant the turn ends, so process_exited races ahead of the daemon's poll of the terminal step-finish and the working→ready settle is truncated; the persistent TUI outlives the settle window so the daemon records working→ready before teardown. Phases assert the canonical ready→working→ready arc where the FINAL ready comes from the length-aborted turn. session_birth is anchored to recording start and left UNPINNED so a transient proc-<PID> presession can't steal identity and cascade same_session_as failures; turn1_start (matching working) establishes the session identity turn1_aborted chains off. The `no state_transition to waiting` invariant holds because the errored turn never reaches a tool/permission gate.

text: Session appears in ready within 2s of agent launch; never enters waiting
PID bound to the ses_… session
User prompt drives the ses_… session ready → working
The turn aborts by error and the session settles back to ready on the same ses_… session — it does not stick in working
